lesson.title: 6 easy steps for starting international trade 2021 (import/export)

videos.title: 6 easy steps for starting international trade 2021 (import/export)

vocabularies.definition: Goods or services brought into a country from another country.
examples: The country's imports of oil have increased.
Imports are essential for many industries.
vocabularies.contextSentence: Imports are any good or service brought in from one country to another

vocabularies.definition: Goods or services produced in one country and sold to another.
examples: The country's exports include manufactured goods and agricultural products.
Increased exports can boost a country's economy.
vocabularies.contextSentence: while exports are goods and services produced in the home country for sale to other markets

vocabularies.definition: Involving many different and connected parts; intricate.
examples: The instructions were complex and difficult to understand.
The global economy is a complex system.
vocabularies.contextSentence: The modern system of international trade is a complex web of import/export businesses that

vocabularies.definition: The action of sharing something out among a number of recipients.
examples: The company handles the distribution of its products.
Efficient distribution is crucial for success.
vocabularies.contextSentence: handle the sale, distribution, and delivery of goods from one nation to another

vocabularies.definition: The quality of being trusted and believed in.
examples: The company needs to build its credibility.
His credibility was damaged by the scandal.
vocabularies.contextSentence: it is all about proving that your organization is trustable

vocabularies.definition: Made lawful; given legal status.
examples: The government legalized the use of cannabis.
The company's activities must be legalized to operate.
vocabularies.contextSentence: in order to get any certification any license to make your activities legalized

vocabularies.definition: Having or showing strong feelings or enthusiasm for something.
examples: She is passionate about environmental issues.
He is passionate about his work.
vocabularies.contextSentence: well, find a product or industry you are passionate about and that you think could sell in international markets

vocabularies.definition: To recognize or establish; to determine.
examples: The police are trying to identify the suspect.
We need to identify the problem before we can solve it.
vocabularies.contextSentence: after finding the right product, identify the right market

vocabularies.definition: Of great importance; essential.
examples: This decision is crucial to our success.
A good education is crucial for a successful career.
vocabularies.contextSentence: A strong relationship with a manufacturer/supplier is crucial to long-running success in an imports/exports business.

vocabularies.definition: The detailed organization and implementation of a complex operation.
examples: The company has a strong logistics department.
The logistics of the project were very challenging.
vocabularies.contextSentence: and figure out the logistics of taking their product from their local warehouse to another one, maybe other side of the globe

vocabularies.definition: The amount added to the cost price of goods to cover overhead and profit.
examples: The retailer added a 50% markup to the product's cost.
The company needs to adjust its markup to remain competitive.
vocabularies.contextSentence: Be sure to price your product such that your markup wont exceed what a customer is willing to pay

vocabularies.definition: A person or company that supplies goods to retailers.
examples: The company uses distributors to sell its products.
Finding reliable distributors is key to market penetration.
vocabularies.contextSentence: You usually need to find distributors and clients who will take on your product and sell to others

vocabularies.definition: The act of organizing people or things so that they work together effectively.
examples: The project required a high degree of coordination.
Effective coordination is essential for success.
vocabularies.contextSentence: it requires an extraordinary amount of coordination

vocabularies.definition: A company that organizes shipments for individuals or corporations to get goods from the manufacturer or producer to a market, customer or final point of distribution.
examples: The company hired a freight forwarder to handle the shipping.
A freight forwarder can simplify the import/export process.
vocabularies.contextSentence: Hiring a global freight forwarder is generally a good idea for all imports/exports businesses,

grammars.title: Passive Voice
grammars.explanation: Used to describe actions where the subject is acted upon. Useful for describing processes and avoiding personal pronouns.
examples: The product is sold internationally.
The goods were delivered on time.
grammars.contextSentence: exports are goods and services produced in the home country for sale to other markets

grammars.title: Present Perfect
grammars.explanation: Used to describe actions that started in the past and continue to the present or have a result in the present.
examples: I have studied SEO ranking.
We have identified the problem.
grammars.contextSentence: For nearly as long as there‚Äôs been people, there‚Äôs been trade.

grammars.title: Modal Verbs of Obligation (have to)
examples: You have to get legalized.
They have to follow the rules.

grammars.title: Conditional Sentences (Type 1)
grammars.explanation: Used to talk about possible situations and their likely results.
examples: If you study SEO, you will rank higher.
If you find the right product, you will succeed.
grammars.structure: If + present simple, will + base verb
grammars.contextSentence: if you just remember I told you that international trade is all about credibility

grammars.title: Relative Clauses
grammars.explanation: Used to provide additional information about a noun.
examples: The product which you can internationally trade.
The company that handles distribution.
grammars.contextSentence: Once you have a product which you can internationally trade, its time for you to find a supplier or a local manufacturer that can make the product

grammars.title: Gerunds and Infinitives
grammars.explanation: Used to express the purpose or reason for an action.
examples: In order to get any certification.
Starting a business in the international trade field.
grammars.contextSentence: in order to get any certification any license to make your activities legalized

grammars.title: Adverbial Clauses of Purpose
grammars.explanation: Used to explain the reason for an action.
examples: In order to find your product.
In order to move your cargo.
grammars.contextSentence: in order to find your product and also do your market research

lesson.notesContent:
# Tariffs ‚ÄĒ Ghi ch√ļ hŠĽćc tŠļ≠p (Band 6.0)

## TŠĽę vŠĽĪng quan trŠĽćng

### imports `/ňą…™mp…Ēňźrts/`
**Nghń©a:** Goods or services brought into a country from another country.
**DŠĽčch:** h√†ng nhŠļ≠p khŠļ©u
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The country's imports of oil have increased.*

### exports `/ňą…õksp…Ēňźrts/`
**Nghń©a:** Goods or services produced in one country and sold to another.
**DŠĽčch:** h√†ng xuŠļ•t khŠļ©u
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The country's exports include manufactured goods and agricultural products.*

### complex `/ňąk…ímpleks/`
**Nghń©a:** Involving many different and connected parts; intricate.
**DŠĽčch:** phŠĽ©c tŠļ°p
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The instructions were complex and difficult to understand.*

### distribution `/ňĆd…™str…™ňąbjuňź Én/`
**Nghń©a:** The action of sharing something out among a number of recipients.
**DŠĽčch:** ph√Ęn phŠĽĎi
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The company handles the distribution of its products.*

### credibility `/ňĆkred…™ňąb…™l…ôti/`
**Nghń©a:** The quality of being trusted and believed in.
**DŠĽčch:** sŠĽĪ t√≠n nhiŠĽám
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The company needs to build its credibility.*

### legalized `/ňąliňź…°…ôla…™zd/`
**Nghń©a:** Made lawful; given legal status.
**DŠĽčch:** hŠĽ£p ph√°p h√≥a
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The government legalized the use of cannabis.*

### passionate about `/ňąp√¶ É…ôn…ôt …ôňąba ät/`
**Nghń©a:** Having or showing strong feelings or enthusiasm for something.
**DŠĽčch:** ńĎam m√™
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *She is passionate about environmental issues.*

### identify `/a…™ňądent…™fa…™/`
**Nghń©a:** To recognize or establish; to determine.
**DŠĽčch:** x√°c ńĎŠĽčnh
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The police are trying to identify the suspect.*

### crucial `/ňąkruňź É…ôl/`
**Nghń©a:** Of great importance; essential.
**DŠĽčch:** quan trŠĽćng
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *This decision is crucial to our success.*

### logistics `/l…ôňąd í…™st…™ks/`
**Nghń©a:** The detailed organization and implementation of a complex operation.
**DŠĽčch:** hŠļ≠u cŠļßn
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The company has a strong logistics department.*

### markup `/ňąm…Ďňźrk Ćp/`
**Nghń©a:** The amount added to the cost price of goods to cover overhead and profit.
**DŠĽčch:** ńĎ√°nh dŠļ•u
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The retailer added a 50% markup to the product's cost.*

### distributors `/d…™ňąstr…™bj…ôt…ôrz/`
**Nghń©a:** A person or company that supplies goods to retailers.
**DŠĽčch:** nh√† ph√Ęn phŠĽĎi
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The company uses distributors to sell its products.*

## ńźiŠĽÉm ngŠĽĮ ph√°p

### Passive Voice
**CŠļ•u tr√ļc:** `Subject + be (is/are/was/were) + past participle`
Used to describe actions where the subject is acted upon. Useful for describing processes and avoiding personal pronouns.
> The product is sold internationally.

### Present Perfect
**CŠļ•u tr√ļc:** `Subject + have/has + past participle`
Used to describe actions that started in the past and continue to the present or have a result in the present.
> I have studied SEO ranking.

### Modal Verbs of Obligation (have to)
**CŠļ•u tr√ļc:** `Subject + have to + base verb`
Expresses necessity or requirement.
> You have to get legalized.

### Conditional Sentences (Type 1)
**CŠļ•u tr√ļc:** `If + present simple, will + base verb`
Used to talk about possible situations and their likely results.
> If you study SEO, you will rank higher.

### Relative Clauses
**CŠļ•u tr√ļc:** `Who/which/that + verb/clause`
Used to provide additional information about a noun.
> The product which you can internationally trade.

### Gerunds and Infinitives
**CŠļ•u tr√ļc:** `To + infinitive or V-ing`
Used to express the purpose or reason for an action.
> In order to get any certification.

lesson.title: Writing a Basic Form - Web Development

videos.title: Writing a Basic Form - Web Development

vocabularies.definition: a pre-designed document or file that can be used as a starting point for creating new documents or files
examples: The company uses a standard template for all its reports.
I created a template for my resume to make it easier to update.
vocabularies.contextSentence: talking about how to use templates.

vocabularies.definition: a piece of software designed for a specific purpose
examples: This is a web application.
I downloaded a new application for my phone.
vocabularies.contextSentence: to build a few templates in a simple web application.

vocabularies.definition: a function or method that processes a specific request or event in a program
examples: The event handler responds to user clicks.
The handler processes the data from the form.
vocabularies.contextSentence: It's got one handler on slash called main page.

vocabularies.definition: to receive or derive a characteristic or property from a parent class or object
examples: The new class inherits properties from the parent class.
She inherited her love of music from her mother.
vocabularies.contextSentence: I've made my main page handler inherit from a class called Handler, which I defined up here.

vocabularies.definition: a function associated with an object or class
examples: The class has several methods.
The method calculates the average.
vocabularies.contextSentence: this just adds one method called Write.

vocabularies.definition: functions designed to assist other functions or processes, often simplifying complex tasks
examples: We'll create helper functions to handle data validation.
The code uses helper functions to improve readability.
vocabularies.contextSentence: And we'll be adding a few more helper functions, to this handler, as we go.

vocabularies.definition: a text-based interface used to interact with a computer system
examples: I opened the terminal to run the command.
The developer used the terminal to debug the code.
vocabularies.contextSentence: Let's go ahead, and make sure this works. We go to our terminal.

vocabularies.definition: a location on a computer's file system where files and other directories are stored
examples: I created a new directory to organize my files.
The file is located in the root directory.
vocabularies.contextSentence: I'm in the template lesson directory, where these files are.

vocabularies.definition: a value that is passed to a function or method
examples: The function takes two parameters.
The parameter controls the size of the image.
vocabularies.contextSentence: it'll just add that parameter to the URL.

vocabularies.definition: to send a form or data to a server for processing
examples: Please submit the form.
I submitted my application online.
vocabularies.contextSentence: Which means when I submit this form, it'll just add that parameter to the URL.

vocabularies.definition: the rules and structure of a programming language
examples: The code has a syntax error.
Understanding the syntax is crucial for writing code.
vocabularies.contextSentence: This is basically the python syntax for a multi line string.

vocabularies.definition: a string that spans multiple lines in code
examples: Use a multi-line string for long text.
The code uses a multi-line string for HTML.
vocabularies.contextSentence: This is basically the python syntax for a multi line string.

vocabularies.definition: to remember something
examples: I recall the incident clearly.
Can you recall what happened last night?
vocabularies.contextSentence: Now, recall that since we didn't set a method on that form, the default method is going to be GET.

grammars.title: Present Simple for habitual actions and facts
grammars.explanation: Used to describe habits, general truths, repeated actions, or unchanging situations.
examples: I go to the gym every day.
The sun rises in the east.
grammars.contextSentence: It's got one handler on slash called main page.

grammars.title: Use of 'which' for non-defining relative clauses
grammars.explanation: Used to add extra information about a noun in the main clause. The information is not essential to identify the noun.
examples: I went to London, which is a beautiful city.
He failed the exam, which was disappointing.

grammars.title: Use of 'as' to show time or reason
grammars.explanation: Used to indicate that something happens at the same time as something else or to give a reason.
examples: As I was walking home, it started to rain.
As it was raining, we stayed inside.
grammars.contextSentence: As you can see, ,we'll run the app server, like so I, I prefer to run

grammars.title: Conditional Sentences (Type 1)
grammars.explanation: Used to talk about possible situations and their likely results in the future.
examples: If it rains, I will stay home.
If you study hard, you will pass the exam.
grammars.structure: If + present simple, will + base form
grammars.contextSentence: If you want to make a basic form we can just return html directly from our handler here.

grammars.title: Use of 'going to' for future plans
grammars.explanation: Used to express intentions, plans, or predictions based on present evidence.
examples: I am going to visit my family next week.
They are going to start a new project.
grammars.contextSentence: Now in this slide, I'm going to ask you lots of questions like, what happens when you do x or y or whatever.

grammars.title: Use of 'since' to show time
grammars.explanation: Used to indicate a period of time that started in the past and continues to the present.
examples: I have lived here since 2010.
She has been working here since Monday.
grammars.contextSentence: Now, recall that since we didn't set a method on that form, the default method is going to be GET.

lesson.notesContent:
# Tariffs ‚ÄĒ Ghi ch√ļ hŠĽćc tŠļ≠p (Band 6.0)

## TŠĽę vŠĽĪng quan trŠĽćng

### template `/ňąt…õmpl…™t/`
**Nghń©a:** a pre-designed document or file that can be used as a starting point for creating new documents or files
**DŠĽčch:** mŠļęu
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The company uses a standard template for all its reports.*

### application `/ňĆ√¶pl…™ňąke…™ É…ôn/`
**Nghń©a:** a piece of software designed for a specific purpose
**DŠĽčch:** ŠĽ©ng dŠĽ•ng
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *This is a web application.*

### handler `/ňąh√¶ndl…ô(r)/`
**Nghń©a:** a function or method that processes a specific request or event in a program
**DŠĽčch:** tr√¨nh xŠĽ≠ l√Ĺ
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The event handler responds to user clicks.*

### inherit `/…™nňąher…™t/`
**Nghń©a:** to receive or derive a characteristic or property from a parent class or object
**DŠĽčch:** kŠļŅ thŠĽęa
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The new class inherits properties from the parent class.*

### method `/ňąm…õőł…ôd/`
**Nghń©a:** a function associated with an object or class
**DŠĽčch:** ph∆į∆°ng thŠĽ©c
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The class has several methods.*

### helper functions `/ňąhelp…ôr ňąf ĆŇčk É…ônz/`
**Nghń©a:** functions designed to assist other functions or processes, often simplifying complex tasks
**DŠĽčch:** h√†m hŠĽó trŠĽ£
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *We'll create helper functions to handle data validation.*

### terminal `/ňąt…úňźrm…™nl/`
**Nghń©a:** a text-based interface used to interact with a computer system
**DŠĽčch:** thiŠļŅt bŠĽč ńĎŠļßu cuŠĽĎi
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *I opened the terminal to run the command.*

### directory `/d…ôňąrekt…ôri/`
**Nghń©a:** a location on a computer's file system where files and other directories are stored
**DŠĽčch:** th∆į mŠĽ•c
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *I created a new directory to organize my files.*

### parameter `/p…ôňąr√¶m…™t…ôr/`
**Nghń©a:** a value that is passed to a function or method
**DŠĽčch:** tham sŠĽĎ
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The function takes two parameters.*

### submit `/s…ôbňąm…™t/`
**Nghń©a:** to send a form or data to a server for processing
**DŠĽčch:** gŠĽ≠i
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *Please submit the form.*

### syntax `/ňąs…™nt√¶ks/`
**Nghń©a:** the rules and structure of a programming language
**DŠĽčch:** c√ļ ph√°p
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *The code has a syntax error.*

### multi-line string `/ňĆm Ćltiňąla…™n str…™Ňč/`
**Nghń©a:** a string that spans multiple lines in code
**DŠĽčch:** chuŠĽói nhiŠĽĀu d√≤ng
**V√≠ dŠĽ•:** *Use a multi-line string for long text.*

## ńźiŠĽÉm ngŠĽĮ ph√°p

### Present Simple for habitual actions and facts
**CŠļ•u tr√ļc:** `Subject + Verb (base form or -s/-es)`
Used to describe habits, general truths, repeated actions, or unchanging situations.
> I go to the gym every day.

### Use of 'which' for non-defining relative clauses
**CŠļ•u tr√ļc:** `Main clause, which + verb...`
Used to add extra information about a noun in the main clause. The information is not essential to identify the noun.
> I went to London, which is a beautiful city.

### Use of 'as' to show time or reason
**CŠļ•u tr√ļc:** `As + clause`
Used to indicate that something happens at the same time as something else or to give a reason.
> As I was walking home, it started to rain.

### Conditional Sentences (Type 1)
**CŠļ•u tr√ļc:** `If + present simple, will + base form`
Used to talk about possible situations and their likely results in the future.
> If it rains, I will stay home.

### Use of 'going to' for future plans
**CŠļ•u tr√ļc:** `Subject + be (am/is/are) + going to + verb`
Used to express intentions, plans, or predictions based on present evidence.
> I am going to visit my family next week.

### Use of 'since' to show time
**CŠļ•u tr√ļc:** `Since + a point in time`
Used to indicate a period of time that started in the past and continues to the present.
> I have lived here since 2010.
